message:
fix(web): add ARIA attributes to LoadingOverlay for screen reader accessibility

The loading overlay was missing proper ARIA roles and labels, making it
invisible or confusing to screen reader users. Without role="dialog"
and aria-modal, assistive technologies wouldn't announce the overlay
or understand it as a modal context.

Changes:
- Add role="dialog" to indicate modal dialog semantics
- Add aria-modal="true" to signal that interaction is trapped
- Add aria-labelledby="loading-message" to associate the dialog
  with its visible title/message
- Add id="loading-message" to the message element for the reference

This ensures screen readers will:
1. Announce when the loading overlay appears
2. Read the loading message as the dialog's accessible name
3. Understand that background content is temporarily unavailable

Improves WCAG 2.1 compliance for modal dialogs (Success Criterion 4.1.2).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
